data.content:
The build generates the file successfully but never adds it to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is generated but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` items using an `ItemGroup` within the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target, after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. Adding `<Compile Include="..."/>` inside the target (after the file is created) includes it in the compilation item group so the C# compiler picks it up.
